judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very similar and equally strong on (a)-(c); Report 1 additionally identifies the missing global/deploy-level kill-switch and recommends adding one (expectation d), which Report 2 omits, making Report 1 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Line 11-12 is the deciding logic: if the key is absent from the tenant's JSONB `settings` (which is the case for every seeded tenant in `seeds/tenants.json` — e.g. tenant-b has `settings: {}`), the function returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Line 11-12 is the deciding logic: when the key is absent (`.get()` returns `None`), the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` so that `None` (and any falsy/missing value) resolves to `False`, e.g. `return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))` alone ... i.e., simply delete the `is None: return True` special case for this specific function.

report_2.evidence: I would change the default in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` so a missing/`None` value returns `False` (or, to be extra safe, treat only an explicit `True` as enabling sends), matching what the UI already displays.

report_1.evidence: Commit `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, a genuinely low-risk, convenience-only UI preference, with the "default to `True` on missing" pattern — that default is reasonable for that flag ... Commit `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly below it with the identical shape ... the two flags are in different risk categories and warranted different defaults.

report_2.evidence: `git show aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, 2026-07-01) shows `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added immediately after `compact_calendar_view_enabled` and reuses its exact `if ... is None: return True` structure line-for-line. ... the two toggles have very different risk profiles (UI cosmetics vs. automated outbound patient communication), and the default-on pattern that's harmless for one is risky for the other.

report_1.evidence: Confirm there's no env-level master switch — there currently isn't one (grep found none), meaning the per-tenant flag is the *only* gate. ... consider whether a global kill-switch/master flag should AND with the per-tenant value, so the whole reminders job can be paused instantly without touching every tenant's settings.
